title: A Participant-Derived Xenograft Mouse Model for HIV Control and Immunotherapy Evaluation

steps.title: NOD
steps.instruction: NOD.Cg- Prkdc scid Il2rg tm1Wjl /SzJ mice (The Jackson Laboratory, strain: 005557, 6–8 weeks of age), hereafter referred to as NSG mice

steps.title: Tenofovir (TDF), Emtricitabine (FTC), and Dolutegravir...
steps.instruction: Tenofovir (TDF), Emtricitabine (FTC), and Dolutegravir (DTG) antiretrovirals (see Recipes) Recipes

steps.title: D10 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or...
steps.instruction: D10 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume DMEM n/a 1,000 mL FBS 10% 100 mL HEPES 1% 10 mL Penicillin/streptomycin 1% 10 mL

steps.title: R10 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or...
steps.instruction: R10 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume RPMI-1640 n/a 1,000 mL FBS 10% 100 mL HEPES 1% 10 mL L-Glutamine 1% 10imL Penicillin/streptomycin 1% 10 mL

steps.title: R10-50 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or...
steps.instruction: R10-50 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume RPMI-1640 n/a 1,000 mL FBS 10% 100 mL HEPES 1% 10 mL L-Glutamine 1% 10imL Penicillin/streptomycin 1% 10 mL IL-2 50 units/mL n/a

steps.title: MACS buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: MACS buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume DPBS n/a 1,000 mL FBS 20% 20 mL EDTA 2 mM n/a

steps.title: Freezing media Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Freezing media Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume FBS 90% 0.9 mL DMSO 10% 0.1 mL

steps.title: Tenofovir (TDF), Emtricitabine (FTC), and Dolutegravir...
steps.instruction: Tenofovir (TDF), Emtricitabine (FTC), and Dolutegravir (DTG) antiretroviral (ARV) cocktail Prepare 15% Kleptose HPB (w/w) in water. Add 80 mL of 15% Kleptose to a volumetric flask and stir in 250 mg of DTG-free base. Stir and sonicate for 30 min or until soluble. Add 510 mg of TDF and 4,000 mg of FTC to the same flask and stir for 3 min. Fill to 100 mL with 15% Kleptose. Confirm the pH is 4.2, filter sterilize through a 0.22 μM filter, and store aliquots at -20 °C until ready for use. Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume 15% Kleptose HPB (w/w) n/a 100 mL TDF 5.1 mg/mL 510 mg FTC 40.0 mg/mL 4,000 mg DTG 2.5 mg/mL 250 mg Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Plate 7
steps.instruction: Plate 7.5 × 10 6 HEK293T cells in 20 mL of DMEM in a T75 flask to obtain a confluency of 80%–85% the next day. Incubate the cells at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 overnight (ON).

steps.title: Prepare transfection master mix by diluting...
steps.instruction: Prepare transfection master mix by diluting 48 μL of Fugene6 dropwise in 600 µL of Opti-MEM and incubate for 5 min at room temperature (RT). Add the entire volume dropwise to a new tube containing 16 µg of full-length HIV JR-CSF plasmid (pYK-JRCSF) and incubate for 20 min at RT before transferring the mix dropwise to the cells. Incubate the cells at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 ON.

steps.title: Remove the media and add 20...
steps.instruction: Remove the media and add 20 mL of fresh D10 to the T75 flask. Incubate the cells at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 ON.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant into a 50...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant into a 50 mL Falcon tube and spin at 400× g for 10 min.

steps.title: Filter the supernatants through a 0
steps.instruction: Filter the supernatants through a 0.45 μm filter into a new 50 mL Falcon tube.

steps.title: Add PEG-it to the filtered supernatants...
steps.instruction: Add PEG-it to the filtered supernatants in a 1:5 dilution and incubate at 4 °C ON (>12 h).

steps.title: To pellet the virus particles, centrifuge...
steps.instruction: To pellet the virus particles, centrifuge the supernatants at 1,500× g at 4 °C for 30 min and aspirate the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 20 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 20 mL of R10 and store aliquots of the virus at -80 °C until use. B. Determination of viral titer by TZM-bl assay Note: This protocol was described in [15].

steps.title: Add 100 μL of D10 into...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of D10 into the wells of a black 96-well plate.

steps.title: Add 25 μL of HIV stock...
steps.instruction: Add 25 μL of HIV stock into the first column of wells and make a 5-fold dilution series of the virus.

steps.title: To each well, add 1 ×...
steps.instruction: To each well, add 1 × 10 4 TZM-bl cells in 100 μL of D10 supplemented with DEAE dextran at a final concentration of 30 μg/mL.

steps.title: Incubate the plate at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 for 48 h.

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant and wash the...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant and wash the cells in 200 μL of PBS per well.

steps.title: Dilute 5× cell culture lysis reagent...
steps.instruction: Dilute 5× cell culture lysis reagent to 1× in distilled water and add 30 μL per well. Shake the plate for 15 min.

steps.title: Add 50 μL of luciferase substrate...
steps.instruction: Add 50 μL of luciferase substrate to each well and incubate at RT for 2 min. Measure the luminescence using a SpectraMax i3x.

steps.title: Determine the TCID50 of the virus using the TCID50 macros
steps.instruction: Determine the TCID50 of the virus using the TCID50 macros. Notes:

steps.title: TCID50 macros can be downloaded from hcv
steps.instruction: TCID50 macros can be downloaded from hcv.lanl.gov ( https://hcv.lanl.gov/content/nab-reference-strains/html/TCID501.xls ).

steps.title: mCD4+ T cells in mice are...
steps.instruction: mCD4+ T cells in mice are infected with 10,000 TCID50 HIV JRCSF in a total volume of 100 μL of R10 via intraperitoneal (IP) injection (see section H). Thus, the virus stock must have a titer of a minimum of 100,000 TCID

steps.title: Thaw cryopreserved PBMCs and resuspend in...
steps.instruction: Thaw cryopreserved PBMCs and resuspend in MACS buffer to a concentration of 5 × 10 7 cells/mL. Note: Use of human PBMCs requires prior ethical approval by an institutional review board and participant informed consent.

steps.title: Isolate mCD4+ T cells by negative...
steps.instruction: Isolate mCD4+ T cells by negative selection using EasySep TM Human Memory CD4+ T-cell Enrichment kit according to the manufacturer’s protocol.

steps.title: Resuspend the enriched mCD4+ T cells...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the enriched mCD4+ T cells to a concentration of 2–5 × 10 6 cells/mL in R10-50 and transfer the cells to an appropriately sized culture flask.

steps.title: Rest the cells at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Rest the cells at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 for a minimum of 5 h or ON before preparing the cells for engraftment.

steps.title: During incubation, assess the purity of...
steps.instruction: During incubation, assess the purity of the enriched mCD4+ T cells by staining 50 μL of cell suspension for flow cytometric analysis using a viability marker and the following anti-human surface marker antibodies: CD3, CD4, CD8, CD45RO, CD56, CD14, CD16, and CD

steps.title: Critical
steps.instruction: Critical : The purity of the enriched mCD4+ T cells should be at least 95% to proceed with engraftments. If a purity of 90%. Lung cell viability is <30% due to cell death caused by CO 2 euthanasia .

steps.title: As described by Jackson Laboratories, lymphoid...
steps.instruction: As described by Jackson Laboratories, lymphoid cell follicles in the spleen and cystic structures in the thymus are absent in NSG mice. There is also diminished cellularity of lymph nodes [19].

steps.title: Use cells immediately for flow cytometric...
steps.instruction: Use cells immediately for flow cytometric analysis or other in vitro assays. Cells can also be cryopreserved in freezing media and stored in liquid nitrogen.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol or parts of it has been used and validated in the following research article(s): McCann et al. [14]. A participant-derived xenograft model of HIV enables long-term evaluation of autologous immunotherapies. J Exp Med. Gramatica et al. [18]. The EZH2 inhibitor tazemetostat mitigates HIV immune evasion, reduces reservoir formation, and promotes durable CD8+ T-cell revitalization. bioRxiv. Figure 1. mCD4+ and mCD8+ T-cell expansion following tail IV engraftment. A. mCD4+ T-cell counts 6 weeks after mCD4+ T-cell engraftment. B. mCD8+ T-cell counts 5 weeks post mCD8+ T-cell engraftment. C. Plasma HIV-1 RNA concentrations 5 weeks post simultaneous mCD8+ T-cell engraftment and HIV infection.
